basic_details.job_title: GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for Teaching Assistant, Steno Typist and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Gandhigram Institute of Rural Health and Family Welfare Trust (GIRHFWT)
basic_details.post_name: Teaching Assistant, Librarian, Projectionist / Project Coordinator, Steno Typist, Upper Division Clerk / Junior Accountant
basic_details.short_information: The Gandhigram Institute of Rural Health and Family Welfare Trust (GIRHFWT) has officially announced the Teaching Assistant, Librarian, Projectionist / Project Coordinator, Steno Typist, Upper Division Clerk / Junior Accountant Recruitment 2026 for a total of 6 vacancies under Advertisement No. GIRH/ESTT/01/0011/2026-2027. The detailed notification was published on the official website at https://www.girhfwt.org. Candidates who meet the prescribed age criteria of 25 to 45 years (Teaching Assistant) and 25 to 35 years (for other posts) along with the required educational qualifications can subm

age_limit.age_details: Min: 25 Years, Max: 45 Years (for Teaching Assistants), Min: 25 Years, Max: 35 Years (for other posts)
age_limit.details: Teaching Assistant - I & II: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 45 Years. Librarian: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years. Projectionist / Project Coordinator: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years. Steno Typist: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years. Upper Division Clerk / Junior Accountant: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years.

qualification.education_qualification: M.Sc. in Biostatistics / Epidemiology / Statistics with 3 years of experience OR M.Sc. in Public Health / M.A. in Social Work / M.A. in Sociology / M.Sc. in Psychology / Master's in Social Science with 3 years of experience OR Any Degree with MPH and 5 years of experience. (Other posts have different qualifications)
qualification.details: Teaching Assistant - I: M.Sc. in Biostatistics / Epidemiology / Statistics with 3 years of experience. Teaching Assistant - II: Any Degree with MPH (Master of Public Health) and 5 years of experience in Teaching / Field Work. OR M.Sc. in Public Health / M.A. in Social Work / M.A. in Sociology / M.Sc. in Psychology / Master's in Social Science with 3 years of experience in Teaching / Field Work. Librarian: Graduated in Arts/Science with B.L.I.Sc. (or) M.L.I.Sc., along with not less than 5 years of experience in the respective field in reputed institutions or organizations. Projectionist / Project Coordinator: A Bachelor's Degree in Civil or Electrical Engineering. Certificate in Computer systems. A minimum of three years of relevant experience in building, electrical, and general office management. Desirable MBA. Steno Typist: Any Degree and Certification in any recognized Computer Course and 2 years of work experience in Secretarial work. Upper Division Clerk / Junior Accountant: A Post Graduate in Commerce and Knowledge of Computer Operation and using of Tally Software is mandatory with not less than 3 years of experience in the Finance (or) Accounts domain. OR A Graduate in Commerce and Knowledge of Computer Operation and using of Tally Software is mandatory with not less than 5 years of experience in Finance or Accounts domain.

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs.75,000/- (for Teaching Assistant), PB1,19500 - 62000, Level - 8 (for Librarian & Projectionist/Project Coordinator), PB1, 20600 - 65500 Level 10 (for Steno Typist & Upper Division Clerk/Junior Accountant)
salary_details.teaching assistant - i & ii: Consolidated monthly pay of Rs.75,000/- on a fixed-term basis.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for GIRHFWT Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: www.girhfw.org.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 19-07-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

content_sections.heading: GIRHFWT Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates who are interested in applying for the GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026 should first download and carefully read the official notification PDF. The notification contains complete details about vacancy distribution, eligibility criteria, age limit, stipend/salary, selection process, important dates, and application instructions. Reading the official document is important to ensure that you meet all requirements before submitting the application form. Download GIRHFWT Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Teaching Assistant - I: M.Sc. in Biostatistics / Epidemiology / Statistics with 3 years of experience. Teaching Assistant - II: Any Degree with MPH (Master of Public Health) and 5 years of experience in Teaching / Field Work. OR M.Sc. in Public Health / M.A. in Social Work / M.A. in Sociology / M.Sc. in Psychology / Master's in Social Science with 3 years of experience in Teaching / Field Work. Librarian: Graduated in Arts/Science with B.L.I.Sc. (or) M.L.I.Sc., along with not less than 5 years of experience in the respective field in reputed institutions or organizations. Projectionist / Project Coordinator: A Bachelor's Degree in Civil or Electrical Engineering. Certificate in Computer systems. A minimum of three years of relevant experience in building, electrical, and general office management. Desirable MBA. Steno Typist: Any Degree and Certification in any recognized Computer Course and 2 years of work experience in Secretarial work. Upper Division Clerk / Junior Accountant: A Post Graduate in Commerce and Knowledge of Computer Operation and using of Tally Software is mandatory with not less than 3 years of experience in the Finance (or) Accounts domain. OR A Graduate in Commerce and Knowledge of Computer Operation and using of Tally Software is mandatory with not less than 5 years of experience in Finance or Accounts domain.

content_sections.heading: GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Teaching Assistant - I & II: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 45 Years. Librarian: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years. Projectionist / Project Coordinator: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years. Steno Typist: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years. Upper Division Clerk / Junior Accountant: Minimum Age: 25 Years, Maximum Age: 35 Years.

rows.salary: Consolidated monthly pay of Rs.75,000/- on a fixed-term basis.

rows.event: Last date for submission of applications

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates who wish to apply for GIRHFWT Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below. Read the official notification carefully before applying. Download the application form from the official website: www.girhfw.org. Fill the application form carefully with correct information. Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates. Check all details carefully before submission. Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed. Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 19-07-2026. Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for submission of applications for the Gandhigram Institute of Rural Health and Family Welfare Trust recruitment is 19th July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in the GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 6 vacancies announced for various posts, including Teaching Assistant, Librarian, Projectionist/Project Coordinator, Steno Typist, and Upper Division Clerk/Junior Accountant.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application mode for the GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026 is offline. Candidates need to download the application form and submit it via post.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for Teaching Assistant posts?
faq.answer: For Teaching Assistant posts, candidates need an M.Sc. in Biostatistics/Epidemiology/Statistics with 3 years of experience, or an M.Sc. in Public Health/M.A. in Social Work/Sociology/Psychology/Master's in Social Science with 3 years of experience, or any degree with MPH and 5 years of experience.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for Teaching Assistant posts?
faq.answer: The age limit for Teaching Assistant posts is between 25 years and 45 years.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary offered for Teaching Assistant posts?
faq.answer: Teaching Assistant posts offer a consolidated monthly pay of Rs. 75,000/- on a fixed-term basis.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for the GIRHFWT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee for all categories is Rs. 1,000/-.

faq.question: Q8. Where can I download the application form?
faq.answer: The application form can be downloaded from the official website of the Gandhigram Institute of Rural Health and Family Welfare Trust, which is www.girhfw.org.

faq.question: Q9. What documents are required to be submitted with the application form?
faq.answer: Candidates need to attach self-attested copies of testimonials, educational certificates, experience certificates, community certificates, and other required documents as mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q10. What is the selection process for these posts?
faq.answer: The selection process for the advertised posts will be based on an interview.
